title: Central Secrets Vault
description: Use Vault/Secrets Manager/Key Vault with rotation; best practice for production.

cost.costNotes: Seeded via heuristics; validate against current pricing and expected workload (throughput, storage, retention, egress).

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Min $0 represents free tier usage (AWS Secrets Manager: 40K API calls free/month, Azure Key Vault: 10K ops free, GCP Secret Manager: 6 free secrets). Max $5000 covers enterprise production scenarios: AWS Secrets Manager at scale ($0.40/secret/month + $0.05/10K API calls, supporting 1000+ secrets with high rotation and 100M+ API calls/month), or HashiCorp Vault Enterprise self-hosted (infrastructure costs: 3-5 HA nodes at $200-300/node + load balancer ~$30 + storage ~$100 + bandwidth ~$50) plus potential enterprise license costs. Mid-range production typically runs $200-500/month for managed services with moderate secret counts (100-500 secrets) and API call volumes (1-10M calls/month).
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-high complexity implementation. Requires: (1) Architectural planning for secret access patterns and rotation policies (8-16 hours), (2) Infrastructure setup including HA configuration, backup/DR, and network security (16-24 hours), (3) Integration with existing applications and CI/CD pipelines (24-40 hours), (4) Implementing secret rotation automation and policies (16-24 hours), (5) Team training on secret management best practices (8-16 hours), (6) Migration of existing secrets with zero-downtime strategy (16-24 hours). Total effort: 88-144 hours. At blended rate of $75-100/hour for DevOps/platform engineers (reduced with AI coding assistants for integration code and policy templates), estimated $3000-4000. Complexity driven by cross-cutting nature, security requirements, and need for coordination across multiple teams and applications.
provenance.licenseCost: Open source and managed cloud options available at $0 license cost. HashiCorp Vault OSS is free (MPL 2.0 license), AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, and GCP Secret Manager are pay-as-you-go managed services with no upfront licensing. However, HashiCorp Vault Enterprise costs $150K-500K+ annually for larger deployments (1000+ nodes, enterprise features like HSM integration, MFA, replication), but OSS version covers most production needs. Cloud-agnostic implementation can use free OSS tools, making baseline license cost $0. Organizations requiring enterprise support, SLA guarantees, or advanced features would incur additional costs as part of monthly operational expenses rather than one-time licenses.

provides.reasoning: This pattern provides centralized secrets management functionality including storage, rotation, and access control using services like HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, or Azure Key Vault.
The pattern explicitly implements automatic rotation capabilities as a core feature, distinguishing it from basic secrets storage solutions.
This pattern provides secure storage for credentials, API keys, tokens, and certificates, which is a fundamental capability distinct from general configuration management.
Production-grade vault solutions inherently encrypt stored secrets at rest, which is a standard security requirement for compliance.
Vault solutions use TLS/SSL to encrypt communication between applications and the vault service, ensuring secrets are protected during transmission.
Production vault solutions track all access to secrets for compliance and security monitoring, providing detailed audit trails of who accessed what and when.

requires.reasoning: A central secrets vault requires underlying infrastructure to run on, including cloud provider resources, compute instances, and networking components.
The vault requires authentication mechanisms such as IAM roles, service accounts, or identity providers to control access to secrets.
Applications and services need network access to communicate with the vault, requiring proper network configuration, firewall rules, and potentially VPN or service mesh connectivity.

supports_nfr.reason: Pattern availability ceiling limited to 99.99% due to vault service dependency and API call overhead.
Pattern adds minimum 50ms p95 latency floor for vault API calls and secret retrieval operations.
Pattern p99 latency floor includes vault authentication, secret retrieval, and occasional rate limiting.
Pattern enables rapid credential rotation and revocation with RTO of 1 minute for secret recovery.
At very low request rates, vault API latency overhead (50-100ms) may be disproportionate; consider simpler config-based secrets for low-traffic services.
Pattern inherently requires and provides comprehensive audit logging of all secret access operations.
Pattern supports GDPR compliance through encrypted secret storage, access control, and audit logging.
Pattern supports HIPAA compliance through encryption, access logging, and secure credential management.
Pattern supports CCPA compliance through centralized access control and audit logging for secret access.
Pattern supports SOX compliance through centralized secret management and audit trails for financial systems.

supports_constraints.reason: Secrets vault services available on all major clouds (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, GCP Secret Manager, HashiCorp Vault).
Pattern is infrastructure/ops-level providing vault service that can be consumed by SDKs in any language.
Central secrets vaults are infrastructure services consumable by any platform type via REST API or SDK.

vault_provider.description: Secrets management provider. AWS/Azure/GCP managed services offer simpler operations with cloud integration. HashiCorp Vault provides multi-cloud flexibility and advanced features but requires more operational overhead.

rotation_enabled.description: Enable automatic secret rotation. Recommended for production to minimize exposure from compromised credentials, but requires application support for dynamic secret refresh.

rotation_schedule_days.description: Days between automatic secret rotations. Shorter intervals improve security but increase operational complexity and potential for rotation-related outages.

encryption_type.description: Encryption key management strategy. Provider-managed is simplest. Customer-managed KMS provides key lifecycle control. BYOK offers maximum control for compliance requirements.

access_control.description: Authentication method for accessing secrets. IAM-based integrates with cloud identity. Token-based suits HashiCorp Vault. Certificate-based provides mutual TLS authentication for zero-trust environments.

audit_logging.description: Enable comprehensive audit logs of all secret access. Essential for compliance and security monitoring but increases storage costs.
